title: Inspiring the Next Generation of Designers
subtitle: 让年轻人爱上设计的竞赛

paragraphs.en: Recently, a leading design federation in the UK warned that the UK could face a creative skills shortage, after the latest figures revealed a drop of almost 10 percent in students studying Design and Technology. These figures showed that the number of pupils that sat exams in Design and Technology fell 10 percent from 204,788 to 185,279 last year. Chief executive John Kampfner of the Creative Industries Federation thinks that this drop in figures has worrying implications for the skills pipeline in Britain's hugely successful arts and creative industries.
paragraphs.zh: 近日，英国一家顶尖设计联合会警告称，英国可能面临创意技能短缺，因最新数据显示学习设计与技术的学生人数下降了近10%。数据显示，去年参加设计与技术考试的学生人数从204788人降至185279人，降幅达10%。创意产业联合会首席执行官约翰·坎普夫纳认为，这一数字下滑对英国蓬勃发展的艺术与创意产业的技能输送管道造成了令人担忧的影响。

paragraphs.en: He said, 'We already have skills shortages in many jobs such as animation and special effects.' He added: 'Engineering, which requires a similar mix of creative and technical skills, also has recruitment problems.' Many other designers and architects have expressed concern over the future supply of home-grown talent for UK firms, and industry bodies have pointed out that there has been a lack of roots-level support. However, if these designers had visited the Design Museum shop in London earlier this year their fears may have been eased.
paragraphs.zh: 他说：“我们在动画和特效等许多岗位上已经存在技能短缺。”他补充道：“工程学同样需要创造性与技术性技能的融合，也存在招聘难题。”许多其他设计师和建筑师也对英国企业未来本土人才供应表示担忧，行业机构则指出缺乏基层支持。然而，如果这些设计师今年早些时候去过伦敦设计博物馆的商店，他们的忧虑或许会有所缓解。

paragraphs.en: With £10 to spend, they could have bought a fascinating children's toy called 'Dazzle Racer'. An automotive cylinder, it included a wind-up, elastic-band-powered mechanism, minimal parts, all 100 percent recycled, and lots of stickers. It was good fun, simple, eye-catching and very original. Well, you'd expect the Design Museum to commission and make some interesting items, but this one was different. Designed by a group of six Year 9 and Year 10 boys from Finchley Catholic High School, the toy was the winning entry in the museum's 2015 Design Ventura competition.
paragraphs.zh: 花10英镑，他们就能买到一款迷人的儿童玩具“炫彩赛车”。它是一个汽车圆筒造型，配有发条和橡皮筋动力装置，零件极少且全部可回收，还附赠许多贴纸。它好玩、简单、吸睛且极富创意。当然，设计博物馆定制出品一些有趣物件不足为奇，但这件与众不同。它由芬奇利天主教高中六名九年级和十年级男生设计，是该博物馆2015年Design Ventura竞赛的获奖作品。

words.meaning: adj. 迷人的，极有吸引力的

paragraphs.en: This competition brings the business of design to life for students aged 13 to 16 by challenging them to develop a new creative, sustainable and commercially feasible product for the Design Museum shop and attracts hundreds of entries nationwide. 'We did Design Ventura in my previous school,' says Liam Hourican, Finchley Catholic High School's design technology curriculum leader. 'Then when I changed schools four years ago, I introduced it here because it helps to develop so many skills.'
paragraphs.zh: 该竞赛面向13至16岁学生，要求他们为设计博物馆商店设计一款兼具创意、可持续性和商业可行性的新产品，将设计商业真实呈现，吸引了全国数百份参赛作品。芬奇利天主教高中设计技术课程负责人利亚姆·霍里根说：“我在之前的学校就参加过Design Ventura。四年前我调任到这里，便引入了这项活动，因为它有助于培养多种技能。”

paragraphs.en: The theme for last year's competition was 'Move', and Hourican began with three groups working in lunch breaks and after school before selecting the group with the most innovative idea. Each participating school may submit just one proposal. 'It's the taking part and doing the work which is important,' he says. 'And I never dreamt we'd win.' Catherine Ritman Smith, head of learning at the Design Museum, is expecting around 10,000 participants aged 13 to 16 to take part this year.
paragraphs.zh: 去年竞赛主题是“动”，霍里根起初让三个小组利用午休和课后时间创作，随后选出创意最佳的一组。每所参赛学校只能提交一份方案。他说：“重在参与和过程。我做梦也没想到我们会赢。”设计博物馆学习主管凯瑟琳·里特曼·史密斯预计今年约有1万名13至16岁学生参赛。

paragraphs.en: She says that the project — funded by Deutsche Bank as part of its youth engagement programme Born To Be — is the biggest event in the museum's calendar. 'We started Design Ventura with around 800 young people in 2010 and it has grown steadily since,' she says. 'Teachers like it because the skills are all transferable and it helps to validate the value of design as a subject,' she explains, telling me that nearly 600 schools have taken part so far. So how does the competition work? A single-word theme is announced in the summer. This year it is 'Change'.
paragraphs.zh: 她表示，该项目由德意志银行资助，是其青年参与计划Born To Be的一部分，也是博物馆日程中最盛大的活动。她说：“我们2010年创办Design Ventura时约有800名年轻人参与，此后稳步增长。老师们喜欢它，因为所学技能均可迁移，且有助于印证设计作为一门学科的价值。”她告诉我至今已有近600所学校参加。那么竞赛如何运作？夏季会公布一个单词主题，今年是“变”。

words.meaning: adj. 可转移的，可迁移的

paragraphs.en: Schools work with groups to produce imaginative design ideas for a product in simple materials that could sell for £10 in the Design Museum shop. Participating schools then register by November and eventually submit their design idea. Along the way there is training and support for teachers and tutoring for students from designers. An additional bonus is when the winners see their design on sale in the spring of the following year.
paragraphs.zh: 各学校组织小组合作，用简单材料为博物馆商店设计一款售价约10英镑的产品，创意需天马行空。参赛学校于11月前注册，最终提交设计方案。过程中会有设计师为教师提供培训支持，为学生进行辅导。额外奖励是获奖者能在次年春天看到自己的设计上架销售。

paragraphs.en: Hourican and his pupils have happy memories of spending a whole day at the museum, having been selected as one of 10 shortlisted schools to present their idea to a panel of judges, including designer Jasper Conran. Then they worked with the Kin Design Studio in Shoreditch, met designers at their school and attended a lunch with Deutsche Bank employees. The students helped to make decisions and there were plenty of discussions — they changed the product's name, for example.
paragraphs.zh: 霍里根和学生们对在博物馆度过的整天记忆犹新，当时他们入围十强，向包括设计师贾斯珀·康兰在内的评审团展示方案。之后他们与肖尔迪奇的Kin设计工作室合作，在学校与设计师会面，还参加了德意志银行员工的午餐会。学生们参与决策，讨论热烈——比如他们更改了产品名称。

paragraphs.en: 'One of the really useful things about this competition,' says Hourican, 'is that there's a commercial element because the product is going on sale for real and the boys had to learn about budgeting and marketing, as well as designing their game.' Profits go to charity. 'The winning team chooses where it wants the profits to go,' says Ritman Smith. 'The Finchley Catholic High School product raised about £1,000 for Great Ormond Street Hospital.'
paragraphs.zh: 霍里根说：“这场比赛真正有用的一点是其中的商业元素，因为产品真的要销售，男孩们不仅要设计游戏，还得学习预算和营销。”利润捐给慈善机构。里特曼·史密斯说：“获胜团队可以选择利润去向。芬奇利天主教高中的产品为大奥蒙德街医院筹集了约1000英镑。”

paragraphs.en: Winning entries in past years have included a make-your-own cloth kit called Dove Bunting and a threefold, wallet-sized travel game with goals at each end, called Badoiiing. It costs the schools nothing to participate in Design Ventura; the professional designers provide their services for free. Among them is architect Asif Khan, a Design Museum trustee who has worked as designer-in-residence at the museum, helping emerging architects. He has recently been commissioned to design the new Museum of London building in Smithfield.
paragraphs.zh: 往年获奖作品包括自制布艺套装“和平鸽彩旗”，以及钱包大小的三折旅行游戏“Badoiiing”，两端设有球门。学校参与Design Ventura无需任何费用，专业设计师免费提供服务。其中包括建筑师阿西夫·汗，他是设计博物馆受托人，曾任驻馆设计师帮助新锐建筑师，最近还受委托设计史密斯菲尔德的新伦敦博物馆大楼。

words.meaning: adj. 新兴的，崭露头角的

paragraphs.en: Another is television presenter and interior designer Naomi Cleaver, who is one of the competition's judges. 'I'm very keen to encourage young people,' she says, observing that design brings together other subjects such as literature, art, history, geography and science. 'Now that design education is limited in the curriculum, I'm all in favour of competitions such as Design Ventura, which helps to bring the standard curriculum to life,' says Cleaver. 'And the judging day is terrific fun. Some of these students are very imaginative.'
paragraphs.zh: 另一位是电视主持人兼室内设计师娜奥米·克利弗，她是比赛评委之一。她说：“我非常热衷于鼓励年轻人。”她观察到设计融合了文学、艺术、历史、地理和科学等其他学科。克利弗说：“如今设计教育在课程中受限，我全力支持Design Ventura这类竞赛，它能让标准课程活起来。而且评审日非常有趣，有些学生极富想象力。”

paragraphs.en: One group presented their idea in the form of a puppet show and we see some marvelous demonstration models. I'm always impressed by the levels of confidence and the support the students get from their teachers. Ritman Smith adds: 'Design technology has become a tricky subject to make a case for, and we've heard of departments closing in some schools. We find that if pupils take part in Design Ventura in Year Nine it can be the trigger which leads them to opt for it at General Certificate of Secondary Education, so we are helping to keep alive something which is crucial to industry and entrepreneurship.'
paragraphs.zh: 有一组以木偶剧形式展示创意，我们还看到了一些精彩的演示模型。学生们表现出的自信和从老师那里获得的支持总是令我印象深刻。里特曼·史密斯补充道：“设计技术已成为一门难以论证其价值的学科，我们听说有些学校的相关院系已关闭。我们发现，如果学生在九年级参加Design Ventura，这可能会促使他们在中等教育普通证书阶段选修该科目。因此，我们正在帮助维系对工业和创业至关重要的东西。”

words.meaning: n. 创业精神，企业家精神
